name: Front
mcp_notes: Front has released an official beta MCP server that allows AI applications to connect to Front data and API documentation.
api_notes: The Front platform features three primary APIs: a Core API (REST) for backend operations, a Plugin SDK for embedding custom UI in Front, and a Channels API for integrating messaging streams. The Core API is extensive, covering most product entities.
one_liner: Front is a customer communication platform that centralizes interactions from multiple channels into a unified, collaborative inbox.
auth_notes: Authentication is handled via API tokens (Bearer tokens). The system supports fine-grained permissions for these tokens. OAuth2 is also supported, particularly for integrations and third-party apps.
access_notes: Developers can sign up for a free developer account at the Front Developer Portal, which provides a sandbox environment to test integrations without affecting production data.
blocker: The Channel API is restricted to authorized partners, which may block the creation of certain types of integrations (like custom application channels) for non-partners.

evidence.claim: Front provides a free developer account for building and testing integrations.
Front's platform consists of a Core API (REST), Plugin SDK, and Channels API.
Front offers an official beta MCP server for AI integrations.
The Channel API is restricted to partners, which can block internal channel integration use cases.
